name: Cafe Au Lait Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan.
3. Heat liqueur and instant coffee in a small saucepan over low heat until the coffee dissolves. Add enough milk to equal 1 cup liquid, then set aside.
4. In a small bowl, whisk together cocoa powder and confectioners' sugar until combined.
5. In a large electric mixer bowl, beat butter until very soft. Gradually add granulated sugar, vanilla extract, and salt, beating until light and creamy.
6. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
7. In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and baking soda.
8. Alternately fold the flour mixture into the butter mixture with the coffee liquid.
9. Pour half of the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the surface.
10. Using the back of a kitchen spoon or teaspoon, make an indentation about 1/2 inch deep in the batter.
11. Spoon the cocoa-confectioners' sugar mixture into the depression.
12. Spoon the remaining batter over the top of the cocoa mixture.
13. Using the broad side of a dinner knife, cut through the batter from bottom to top in a zigzag pattern to create a marbled effect.
14. Smooth the batter surface with the back of a spoon.
15. Bake for 50-55 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
16. Remove the cake from the oven and let it cool in the pan on a rack for 15 minutes.
17. Run a knife around the center of the tube pan and along the outer edge.
18. Carefully invert the cake onto a serving plate and allow it to cool to room temperature before garnishing with confectioners' sugar.
